definition: Earnings revision breadth is a leading indicator with 4-8 month lead time at cycle peaks (8/9 episodes since 1970), but signal quality is degraded by market bifurcation and backtest inflation. Current reading has declined from ~60% to ~45-50%, analogous to Q3 1999 or Q1 2007.
evidence: 8/9 episodes since 1970 with 4-8 month lead times, COVID sole exception
Current aggregate ERB decline from ~60% to ~45-50%
Actionable lead time only 1-3 months (inside institutional rebalancing noise)
Geopolitically-attributed special items may distort signal by ~5-10pp
open_questions: Whether bifurcation between Mag7 and S&P 493 revisions degrades aggregate signal
Whether adjusted ERB (excluding special items) is more informative
Timing precision limitations for institutional implementation
